Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of The Decedents Estate; Petition For Instructions Regarding The Parentage Of Decedent
Off Calendar Jul-10-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Lachona for Leo Bautista (Administrator).

Mr. Lachona stated that he reviewed the notes from the Probate Examiner, that he filed a supplement last night, that his thought is the supplement addresses all of the questions, that he has heard nothing from any Beneficiary objecting and that he believes everything is taken care of. The Petition to Determine Persons Entitled to Distribution of the Decedent's Estate, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of The Decedents Estate; Petition For Instructions Regarding The Parentage Of Decedent

Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of The Decedents Estate; Petition For Instructions Regarding The Parentage Of Decedent
Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Continued To May-01-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Lachona for Leo Bautista (Administrator).

The Court noted that it received documents in Spanish that were not translated. Mr. Lachona stated that those were the documents they received from the Investigator, expressed concern about the costs and time of having hundreds of pages translated, stated that they haven't received any objections, that he thinks they have laid out the family tree sufficiently and that translation will add several months to the estate. Mr. Lachona also stated that Adolfo Avendano is deceased (referred to exhibit 95), that he died in 1990 without any issue and that the addresses for Guadalupe Avendano Lopez's Granddaughters were included in the investigator's report in exhibit B. The Petition to Determine Persons Entitled to Distribution, etc. is continued to May 1, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of The Decedents Estate; Petition For Instructions Regarding The Parentage Of Decedent

Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of The Decedents Estate; Petition For Instructions Regarding The Parentage Of Decedent
Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Continued To Jan-17-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Lachona for Leo Bautista (Administrator).

Mr. Lachona stated that initially they thought there would be objection and that since he filed his petition, he has not heard from those parties. The Court noted that there is a notice issue with respect to notice to the Granddaughters of Juana Guadalupe Avendano Lopez. Mr. Lachona stated that they are represented by Counsel and that he served the Attorney. The Court stated that they are entitled to direct notice and that it does not have supporting documents for the family relationship. Mr. Lachona stated that he is happy to provide them, that they are from family members, the investigator and conversations, that it is a very large binder and that the documents do not cover all of the potential heirs. Mr. Lachona also stated that he feels they have exhausted every resource they could to get information, that he has thought of ways to present it to the Court that might be more easily reviewed and that some of the names differ (surnames, maiden names). The Petition to Determine Persons Entitled to Distribution, etc. is continued to January 17, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for notice, for filing of a supplement and for filing of a declaration of diligence as to tracking down heirs.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Apr-20-2016 Granted Per Written Order

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (3316 19th Street, Sf Ca 94110)
Off Calendar Apr-27-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Lachona & Richard Shu for and with (administrator) Leo Bautista.

The sale was confirmed at $900,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). The amount of the additional bond is to be determined. Mr. Lachona stated that there have been out of pocket expenses in regards to the landlord tenant agreement and the settlement agreement, that they would like the bond to be less those expenses, that the expenses will be paid out immediately and that the bond amount would be $674,000.00. The Court stated that it is not the Court's practice to do that and took the matter under submission.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-23-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathon Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA). Sheila Robello for (petitioner) Leo Bautista. James Bridgman for (petitioner) Irma Intuvisankul and Vilma Castro (heir). Diana Redding (via court call) for (petitioner) Victor Martinez Guevara.

Mr. Ferdon stated that the SFPA has not received notice, copies of documents or notes from the examiner, that he was initially asked to come into the case by Mr. Molinari and that he has spoken with Ms. Castro and does not know if the parties are in agreement to the appointment of a private fiduciary. Mr. Bridgman reported that they filed an objection to the two petitions on 3/19/15, that they have filed a declaration of Vilma Castro in support of Irma Intuvisankul, that Irma is the most logical choice as many family members reside in other countries, that a private fiduciary will not know where the family members are and that Mr. Martinez is an interloper with a burden to prove that he has a stake in this. Ms. Redding reported that the court had previously suggested a neutral party, that she and Mr. Molinari agreed to Leo Bautista and urged the court to appoint a neutral party if the SFPA does not accept. Ms. Robello stated that there has been an ongoing dispute preventing appointment, that she is confident they can find the relatives, that she just received the objections and that they stand by ready to serve. Mr. Bridgman stated that his client was not consulted regarding appointing a neutral person, that it is unfair, that it will waste money and that it is better to have a family member appointed. The Petition for Letters of Administration (Leo Bautista) is granted (subject to review of the order). The Petition for Letters of Administration, Limited Appointment (Irma Intuvisankul) and the Petition for Letters of Administration (Victor Martinez Guevara) are off calendar.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, LIMITED APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-15-2014 Continued To Jan-26-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for Public Administration. Gino Molinari for and with Irma Intuvansankul.

Mr. Molinari stated that his Client objects to appointment of a professional fiduciary, that it will create additional expenses and that they are only dealing with one piece of property. Mr. Ferdon asked the Court not to appoint the Public Administrator today and allow Counsel to send documents to him/provide information and continue the matter. Mr. Ferdon also stated that there are Medi Cal liens for both parties that are substantial, that the Public Administrator can not negotiate the liens and that he will file a petition for the Public Administrator to be appointed. Both petitions for letters of administration were continued to January 26, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, LIMITED APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-20-2014 Continued To Dec-01-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Diana Redding for and with Victor Guevara. Gino Molinari for Joel Rojas (Petitioner). Jonathan Ferdon for Public Administrator.

Mr. Ferdon stated that he did not have much of a chance to review the file, that it is up to the Parties to file what they have done to contact the Cousins and file their instructions as to what they are to do with the real property (taxes, bills, rents). Mr. Molinari stated that the Decedent's Father was an only Child, that there will only be maternal cousins, that his Client was the Attorney-in-Fact which was never revoked and that his Client has priority. Ms. Redding stated that they are in agreement to having a neutral party appointed, that there is a $69,000.00 Medi-Cal claim for the Wife and a $96,000.00 Medi-Cal claim for the Decedent, and that they want the opportunity to submit a creditor's claim for services rendered. The Court stated that it is disinclined to appoint someone other than a neutral and that if the Parties can not agree to a neutral, they will have to file the information requested by Mr. Ferdon. Both Petitions for Letters of Administration were continued to December 1, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Ferdon raised the issue of the Parties negotiating with Medi-Cal.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-20-2014 Continued To Dec-01-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, LIMITED APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-10-2014 Continued To Oct-20-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Diana Redding for Victor Guevara. Gino Molinari for and with Irma Intuvisankul.

The Court asked Counsel if they were interested in a neutral fiduciary. Ms. Redding expressed concern about a neutral party being appointed stating that she is on the fence because the neutral may not pursue the issue of who is entitled to the Estate. Ms. Redding also stated that she contacted the Department of Health Services and that at least $150,000.00 is going to them. Mr. Molinari argued that his Client is best equipped to be the Administrator and avoid a double fee, that they found four siblings, that the primary heir is going to be his Client's Mother and that they are in the process of making contact with the Heirs in Mexico. Mr. Molinari also clarified that Irma is Estela. Both Petitions for Letters of Administration were continued to October 20, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to try and agree on a neutral fiduciary.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
